title: populus nigra subsp. thevestina
intro: Populus nigra subsp. thevestina, also known as Italian black poplar, is a subspecies of black poplar tree native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its distinctive dark bark and narrow leaves. The medicinal properties of this plant lie in its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, making it effective in reducing inflammation and pain. The health benefits of Italian black poplar include its ability to alleviate fever, reduce inflammation, and treat various respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Italian black poplar is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from colds and flu to skin irritations and wounds.
